header: 2Background: From Softmax Attention to Linear Attention

images.caption: Figure 1:Cross-Layer Error Residuals (CLER). A lower routing-capable recurrent-memory layer computes a DeltaNet-style write residual. If softmax-attention layers intervene, the residual is carried forward as a side-channel. The next routing-capable layer scales the routed residual and injects it into its value target before computing its own write residual.

Figure 2:Cross-Layer Value Routing (CLVR). Each routing-capable layer projects its internal write value into the shared residual stream through a zero-initialized projectionPlP_{l}; the routed contribution starts at zero and is added in a depth-aligned space, where it is read by all later layers and the output head.

Figure 3:Validation-loss scaling for the 350M-parameter runs trained to 15B tokens. Lower is better. In this sweep, Kimi Delta Attention with Muon in a hybrid stack reaches the lowest final loss, while DeltaNet and Gated DeltaNet remain competitive with simpler recurrent-memory updates.

Figure 4:Learning-rate ablation for 350M-parameter hybrid models trained for 2000 steps, approximately 1.05B tokens. The preferred learning rate varies by optimizer and mixer, making learning-rate tuning necessary before drawing architectural conclusions.

Figure 5:Learning-rate ablation iso plots for 350M-parameter hybrid models trained for 2000 steps. The iso plots show the final loss interpolation line for different learning rates. The loss increasing on the left and right ends for all attention/optimizer combination implies the covered range is sufficient, reaching from a too-low learning rate over the optimum up to a too-high learning rate. The optimum lies between3×10−43\times 10^{-4}and10−310^{-3}depending on attention/optimizer.

Figure 6:Iteration-time scaling from 4k to 32k tokens. Softmax attention time grows much faster with sequence length (+192%+192\%) than Gated DeltaNet hybrid (+65%+65\%) and pure stacks (+8%+8\%). Note that hybrid iteration time increase is very close to1/31/3of softmax, matching the fact that hybrid has1/31/3softmax layers. Error bars (step-to-step variance) are very low.

header: 7Discussion: Validation Loss, Throughput, and Architectural Trade-offs
